svg.text: Two-Pointer Pattern — Convergent and Parallel Variants
Use when input is sorted or symmetric. Pointers L=0, R=n-1 walk toward each other.
Decision rule: compare A[L]+A[R] (or some predicate) against target.
• too small → L++ • too large → R-- • match → record / shift both
Examples: Two Sum II (sorted), 3Sum, Container With Most Water, Trapping Rain Water,
Valid Palindrome, Reverse String, Sort Colors (Dutch flag — three pointers).
Invariant: the answer (if any) lies in A[L..R]. Each step shrinks the search space by 1.
Time O(n), space O(1). The standard upgrade from O(n²) brute force.
Use for in-place transformations, partitioning, or read/write head separation.
Slow pointer marks the next write position; fast pointer scans forward.
Examples: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array, Move Zeroes, Remove Element,
Partition (Lomuto / Hoare), Merge Sorted Array (write from the back).
Invariant: A[0..slow] holds the "kept" prefix; A[fast..n-1] holds unprocessed.
Sub-pattern: fast/slow on linked lists for cycle detection (Floyd) — see separate article.
